item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You misunderstand. Some of the Misters use inconsistent gender pronouns when talking about Dr. Wondertainment. That's because he/she is an entity, whom in this case took control of O5-4's body when he became Mr. Collector.

O5-4 wasn't always Wondertainment, but the prize for collecting all 20 Misters sounds about as sketchy as the rest of the operation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm having difficulty wrapping my head around how the O5 was Wondertainment. I don't know how he managed to keep his persona after Forgetful wiped away his memories.

item.title: Order of events
item.content:encoded: So, confused. In this document the paper of misters is shown to have collected them all, therefore making it seem like Redd is last. However, Mr. Stripes stripe is not over Mr lie, as it is in every other document. Doesn’t this mean that Mr. Stripe saw Mr. Lie AFTER Redd was discovered???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great story, but I am more than a little upset that you didn't name him "bluu".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe a little confusing at times, but I loved it nonetheless! +1, my friend, I thoroughly enjoyed this tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lived up to the hype.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there's something iv'e been wondering about for a while, with this being a tale and the accepted article for mr. redd, will there ever be an actual mr. redd SCP?

item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Just noting that a recent edit, introducing a comma that was not technically necessary, was cleared by the author (TwistedGears).

Of note, generally stylistic edits that are not correcting for grammar/spelling/mechanics would require author and/or staff permission first. However, in this case, the author is fine with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ A few small mistakes I might have found in the text
- Hide

There was a thing, locked deep in the hole my brothers were buried.

Should be "buried in", I think.

"Not all of you, no." Mister Collector, née Doctor Wondertainment smiled.

"née" is a borrowed French word meaning "born", typically used to denote a woman's maiden name - as such, it is in feminine form. Since you're using the word to describe a male, I would suggest using the masculine form, "né"; of course, "né" is never used in this manner, since men don't have maiden names, so it's really your choice whether or not to change it - you can keep the original (but grammatically incorrect) form, or use the grammatically correct form, even though it's never used this way. (French is my native language)

Foundation personnel stared up in slack-jaw awe.

I think this should be "slack-jawed".

A thin umbilical cord connecting Katherine to her son.

Looking at the previous sentence, and the next, I feel this should be "connected".

You've found them all and became Mr. Collector!!

"become", I think.

As for the tale, I enjoyed it, but became confused at the end when the Scarlet King's spawn showed up - I think I'm simply not familiar enough with the Scarlet King to understand everything that's happening. I'll hold off on voting for now, and come back when I've brushed up on him. This seems like solid work, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The use of 231, and especially her ignominious death, was off-putting enough that I nearly downvoted. I really don't think 231 should be used as a plot device; if she's going to appear, she needs to be centered, or at least given any kind of personal significance at all.

Also, a few bumps in the depictions where things got too predictable (the Mega-Stabbin'-then-Huggin', for example, and the taking-away-the-hate was predictable as well as too easy), but generally I found myself fascinated, and I wouldn't say it was all predictable.

The rest of the story is quite well done. I liked the takes on Mr. Collector and Mr. Redd. Redd was particularly convincing and I liked his POV.

So… Not sure of my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love both this and the entire Little Mister series, here are a couple of errors I spotted:

He gave the top of the bubble a light pat and it collapsed into itself, away for him to take out later.

Great work.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: goddamn Mr. Redd did not disappoint but it makes me wonder why did wondertainment see the scarlet king and say "time to make this sumbitch a kids' toy!".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm gonna go ahead and say yes, because… well, that's the point. My stories all take place within a slightly altered Acidverse. Notice Redd mentions going to the Wonderworks and talking to Isabel, which is how Cool War ends. :P The key difference here is really only that Wondertainment was never Nobody because Nobody being Everybody was dumb and will never not be dumb, and some slight additions to the Isabel/Redd conversation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, did not expect you to post this so quickly.

Like I said when it was a draft, I really liked this tale. It has a rather intimidating length for tales on this site, but the whole time the tension keeps building which makes it a rather quick read. That tension eventually builds to a rather enjoyable payoff as well, with the ending reveal being a wonderful twist.

+1 Good job TG. 10/10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no will always be one of my favorite works on the site, and I could not read this except under its shadow.

Having said that, I still upvoted, as I think this stands well enough as its own continuity. Plus it doesn't directly contradict no or the cool war canon, so one could headcanon this as happening afterwards.

EDIT: Hell, you could even make the case that the last line in the Cool War is foreshadowing this. As Mr. Redd leaves Wondertainment:

He turned and shuffled out the door, trailing scarlet behind him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've already told you my general opinion personally but I'll put it here for the record: I really dig this. When things are built up for so long in the way the Misters are, with Redd as the big question mark, when you finally "reveal the monster" it will usually turn out to be disappointing compared to the hype. So I was really pleasantly surprised with how good this was.

It maintains a fitting, semi-surreal atmosphere that really works great for the piece as a whole, and every connection made to other works feels wholly deserved. Connections to the Scarlet King are the kind of thing that makes me afterwards think "it makes so much sense, why didn't I think of that earlier?", which is pretty much perfect for a reveal.

I could probably go on for longer about why I think this is great, but it'd just tend into rambling: I really really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Redd seems to have taken a page from Able's book. <.< Can't say I dislike the Wondertainment/Scarlet King connections, though.

item.title: Bluee is too cute for me. +1
(no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy how consistently overboard this goes in terms of weirdness and continues to top itself with twists, yet still has momentum throughout and manages not to be boring and does manage to be incredibly entertaining. My one real complaint is that I never really could fully reconcile my thoughts on who Redd really is with tales that are exposition dumps on him (I think yes is the only one that really did it in a way I could get behind, but then this can't really be too compared to that). This manages to at least leave some element of mystery behind, so in that regard, this one still works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good

don't have much word to say for how great it is -Bob Barker

<background> "New York's times best seller"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On fucking point. Thoroughly enjoyed in drafts, enjoyed just as much now.

item.title: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
Here we go, the next part of my little Wondertainment story arc thing. This is the farthest along chronologically, gods know if it'll be the last. Probably not. So look forward to more Wondertainment nonsense from me, the eventually completion of the first Little Mister series of articles, and maybe hopefully a second series if I can get the correct asses in gear.

Thanks to kaktus, Conwell, Randomini, and vezaz for taking a look at this beforehand.
